Research & Papers.title: FAIRVAR: Fair Federated Learning via Variance Regularization

Research & Papers.title: Lane Change Intention Prediction of two distinct Populations using a Transformer

Abstract: Next-token prediction is the self-supervised signal that trains language models, and every observed prompt token provides the same signal at test time. Research & Papers.title: LangMAP: A Language-Adaptive Approach to Tokenization
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2606.23566v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Language-specific tokenizers improve tokenization quality and the downstream performance of models on those languages. Research & Papers.title: Evaluation Awareness Is Not One Capability: Evidence from Open Language Models

arXiv:2606.23583v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Safety benchmarks assume that test-condition behavior predicts deployment behavior, an assumption that fails if models detect evaluation cues and adapt. Research & Papers.title: EnterpriseClawBench: Benchmarking Agents from Real Workplace Sessions

Abstract: Prior work shows that large language models (LLMs) exhibit introspective capability on benign tasks. Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are typically pretrained on short sequences and then extended to work on longer sequences with additional training. Abstract: Production LLMs increasingly rely on toxicity-based moderation filters as a primary defense, assuming that harmful intent correlates with toxic surface wording. Abstract: The open-source ecosystem on GitHub lacks a systematic hierarchical taxonomy of software repositories. Research & Papers.title: Toward Open Weight Models Without Risks: Separating Public and Private Capabilities in LLMs

Abstract: Open-weight Large Language Models (LLMs) enable scientific progress and broad deployment. However, they make it difficult to control access to sensitive capabilities. Abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have shown strong performance in visual and audio understanding when evaluated in isolation. However, their ability to jointly reason over omni-modal (visual, audio, and textual) signals in long and complex videos remains largely unexplored. Research & Papers.title: PROMPT2BOX: Uncovering Entailment Structure among LLM Prompts
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2603.21438v2 Announce Type: replace 
Abstract: To discover the weaknesses of LLMs, researchers often embed prompts into a vector space and cluster them to extract insightful patterns. Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2604.05087v2 Announce Type: replace 
Abstract: Document expansion is a classical technique for improving retrieval quality, and is attractive since it shifts computation offline, avoiding additional query-time processing. However, when applied to modern retrievers, it has been shown to degrade performance, often introducing noise that obfuscates the discriminative signal. Research & Papers.title: A Structured Clustering Approach for Inducing Media Narratives
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2604.10368v2 Announce Type: replace 
Abstract: Media narratives wield tremendous power in shaping public opinion, yet computational approaches struggle to capture the nuanced storytelling structures that communication theory emphasizes as central to how meaning is constructed. Research & Papers.title: Skill-RAG: Failure-State-Aware Retrieval Augmentation via Hidden-State Probing and Skill Routing
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2604.15771v3 Announce Type: replace 
Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has emerged as a foundational paradigm for grounding large language models in external knowledge. Research & Papers.title: StepPO: Step-Aligned Policy Optimization for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2604.18401v4 Announce Type: replace 
Abstract: Agentic reinforcement learning (RL) is emerging as a critical post-training paradigm for improving LLM agent capabilities. Research & Papers.title: Voice of India: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Real-World Speech Recognition in India
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2604.19151v3 Announce Type: replace 
Abstract: Existing Indic ASR benchmarks often use scripted, clean speech and leaderboard driven evaluation that encourages dataset specific overfitting. Research & Papers.title: Evaluating LLM-Driven Summarisation of Parliamentary Debates with Computational Argumentation
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2604.19331v4 Announce Type: replace 
Abstract: Understanding how policy is debated and justified in parliament is a fundamental aspect of the democratic process. Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2604.25130v2 Announce Type: replace 
Abstract: Evaluating long document summaries remains the primary bottleneck in summarization research. Existing metrics correlate weakly with human judgments and produce aggregate scores without explaining deficiencies or guiding improvement, preventing effective refinement in applications requiring verifiable accuracy. Abstract: Evaluation of long-form, citation-backed reports has lately received significant attention due to the wide-scale adoption of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems. Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.30876v2 Announce Type: replace 
Abstract: Diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs) have recently emerged as a promising alternative to autoregressive models, offering competitive performance while naturally supporting parallel decoding. Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.31201v2 Announce Type: replace

arXiv:2606.01336v2 Announce Type: replace 
Abstract: As real-world applications increasingly require processing inputs of 100k+ tokens, the gap between context length and inference efficiency has become a critical bottleneck. Abstract: Code-switching (CS), the alternation between multiple languages within a single utterance, remains challenging for Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR). Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: Making large language models (LLMs) deeply forget specific knowledge and values without sacrificing general capabilities remains a central challenge in unlearning. Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2606.17999v2 Announce Type: replace

Research & Papers.title: When Does Streaming Tool Use Help? Characterizing Tool-Intent Stabilization in Streaming Retrieval-Augmented Generation
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2606.20113v2 Announce Type: replace 
Abstract: Streaming Retrieval-Augmented Generation (Streaming RAG) hides tool latency by issuing retrieval queries in parallel with the user's still-arriving input, before the utterance is complete. Speculation can only help, though, when the correct query becomes determinable before the user stops speaking or typing -- a property of the query, not the system. Platforms & Products.summary: Senators from both parties criticized banks over overdraft fees, though populist Republicans and their more establishment conservative colleagues were split over whether blame for the problem lies with banks conduct or the consumers.

Platforms & Products.title: It's 10 p.m. Do you know where your insurance-company premiums are?
Platforms & Products.summary: Insurance companies are increasingly shifting their assets into offshore vehicles, where they are being used for…what exactly? We don't know, and that's the problem.

Platforms & Products.title: Wisconsin mutual agrees to buy stock-traded rival
Platforms & Products.summary: After encountering a rough patch in 2023, seller 1895 Bancorp in Greenfield, Wisconsin, hired an investment bank to consider strategic opportunities.

Platforms & Products.title: Bank chatbot platform Kasisto acquired by Backbase
Platforms & Products.summary: Kasisto developed one of the first conversational AI chatbots for banks, and the deal brings its technology to Backbase's new AI-based bank operating system.

Platforms & Products.title: Are you a fintech seeking a bank charter? Consider these implications
Platforms & Products.summary: As fintech firms rush to take advantage of a new regulatory attitude toward bank charter applications, their leaders are about to step into a very new compliance environment. They need to understand what they're getting themselves into.

Platforms & Products.title: Talk less, smile more: Behind Warsh's communications gambit
Platforms & Products.summary: The Federal Reserve's new chair wants to change the way the central bank communicates with markets and the public. What those changes ultimately amount to could represent a major shift in an agency that has made transparency a guiding light for decades.

Regulation.title: WhatsApp Is Getting A New CEO From The Fintech World
Regulation.summary: Will Cathcart is stepping down after seven years leading WhatsApp.

Regulation.title: Beijing is summoning executives again, but here's why that's causing less worry than in 2021
Regulation.summary: Beijing is summoning tech executives for meetings as it did in 2021, but now it has less room to wield a heavy hand due to deflation and rivalry with the U.S.
